context_prose: main.go is the process entry point for the engram CLI binary. It contains no business logic: it wires real-OS I/O streams (`os.Stdout`, `os.Stderr`, `os.Stdin`) and `os.Exit` into `cli.SetupSignalHandling`, then forwards the resulting target list into `targ.Main`. By project convention it is excluded from coverage — only re-exports and thin wrappers, no testable logic. All command dispatch, signal-handler installation, and DI wiring is delegated to the `cli` package; this file's only architectural job is to be the boundary at which concrete OS handles enter the program.

edges.label: calls SetupSignalHandling(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, os.Stdin, os.Exit) and forwards targets into targ.Main
execs the binary as a subprocess (Bash tool)

legend_items: **focus** (yellow): the file in scope for this ledger.
**component** (light blue): peer Go components inside the binary.
name: main

properties.name: Single entry point
properties.notes: main is excluded from coverage by project convention; entry behavior is exercised by every CLI integration run.
properties.statement: For all process invocations of the engram binary, control enters at `main.main` and exits only when `targ.Main` returns or a forced-exit signal handler calls `os.Exit`.

properties.name: Concrete I/O wired at the edge
properties.notes: Honors the project's DI-at-the-edge rule: `internal/` packages never call `os.*` directly; the only `os.Stdout/Stderr/Stdin` reference is here.
properties.statement: For all process invocations, the standard I/O handles supplied to the CLI are exactly `os.Stdout`, `os.Stderr`, and `os.Stdin` — no other code path injects real-OS streams into the binary.

properties.name: Real os.Exit is the terminator
properties.notes: Tests cover `SetupSignalHandling` with a stub `exitFn` (signal_test.go); the binding of the real `os.Exit` here is intentionally untested at the unit layer.
properties.statement: For all process invocations, the exit-function passed into the signal-handling setup is `os.Exit` — second-signal force-exit terminates the real process, not a stubbed one.

properties.name: Signal handlers installed before dispatch
properties.notes: Ordering is a single-expression invariant: `SetupSignalHandling(...)` evaluates and returns before `targ.Main` receives its arguments.
properties.statement: For all process invocations, `cli.SetupSignalHandling` is invoked before `targ.Main`, so SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers and the force-exit goroutine are active for the entire lifetime of subcommand execution.

properties.name: No business logic in entry point
properties.notes: Enforced by convention and the L3 catalog row for E20 ("No business logic; excluded from coverage per project convention."). Drift would show up as new statements inside `main`.
properties.statement: For all changes to `cmd/engram/main.go`, the file contains only the entry-point shell: package declaration, imports, and a `main` function that delegates to `cli` and `targ` — no command logic, parsing, or I/O is performed here.
